name: Majestic 12 Full
description: Majestic 12 (MJ-12) is the alleged code name of a secret committee of scientists, military leaders, and government officials, supposedly formed in 1947 in the United States. Proponents believe MJ-12 was created to investigate recovered alien spacecraft and manage information related to extraterrestrial encounters, particularly the Roswell incident. The theory gained prominence in the 1980s with the circulation of purported leaked documents. These documents, often referred to as the "MJ-12 papers," outline the committee's structure, purpose, and activities. Key figures associated with the alleged group include President Harry Truman, various high-ranking military officers, and prominent scientists. The core claim is that the US government has concealed evidence of extraterrestrial life and technology from the public for national security reasons. Mainstream historians and scientists overwhelmingly dismiss the MJ-12 documents as hoaxes. The FBI investigated the documents upon their initial release and declared them to be fraudulent. Common criticisms include inconsistencies in document formatting, signatures that don't match known exemplars, and the lack of corroborating evidence from credible sources. The theory persists within UFO conspiracy circles, fueled by a distrust of government transparency and a desire to believe in extraterrestrial visitation. Skeptics argue that the MJ-12 narrative relies on speculation and misinterpretation of available evidence.
subtitle: Alleged secret UFO management committee

claims.claim: MJ-12 was formed in 1947 to manage UFO information.
claims.criticism: The MJ-12 documents have been widely discredited as forgeries by experts and the FBI.

claims.claim: The US government is concealing evidence of alien contact.
claims.evidence: The Roswell incident, alleged leaked documents, witness testimonies.
claims.criticism: The Roswell incident has mainstream explanations (Project Mogul), leaked documents are likely forgeries, and witness testimonies are subjective and prone to error.

claims.claim: MJ-12 operated under presidential authority and included top officials.
claims.evidence: Signatures and names listed on the alleged MJ-12 documents.
claims.criticism: The signatures and the overall construction of the documents are inconsistent with authentic government documents of the era. The chain of custody is nonexistent.

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who promoted the MJ-12 documents

keyFigures.name: William Moore
keyFigures.role: Ufologist involved in the initial release of the documents

keyFigures.name: Philip J. Klass
keyFigures.role: Skeptical investigator who debunked the MJ-12 documents

timeline.event: Alleged formation of Majestic 12 following the Roswell incident
The MJ-12 documents begin to circulate among UFO researchers
The FBI investigates and declares the MJ-12 documents to be fraudulent

sources.author: Richard Dolan
sources.work: UFOs and the National Security State
